QUARTER SIZED AND SOME SLIGHTLY LARGER STONES REPORTED IN LAKESIDE.
3 INCHES OF RAIN SINCE 945 PM. PINE CREEK IS FLOODING. BRADSHAW ROAD IMPASSIBLE AT CREEK.
AT CAMERON TRADING POST
TRUCK BLOWN OVER ON I-17
AT SUNSET POINT
WATER OVER ROAD ON RT 260 AT MILEPOST 218
LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTED A 3 TO 4 FOOT RISE IN HAVASU CREEK NEAR SUPAI VILLAGE.
A CINDER BLOCK FLEW THROUGH A WALL IN A HOUSE AND POKED A HOLE THROUGH THE SHEET ROCK INSIDE. ANOTHER CINDER BLOCK FELL INTO THE METAL ROOF.

FLOOD WATERS FROM RAIN WHICH FELL EARLY TUESDAY EVENING REACHED SUPAI VILLAGE BETWEEN 230 AND 300 AM. HIGH WATER UP TO SEVERAL FEET IN DEPTH CAUSED TRAILS AND BRIDGES TO BECOME IMPASSABLE...STRANDING SEVERAL HIKERS AND FLOODING AREA CAMPGROUNDS.
4 FOOT RISE IN LESS THAN 30 MINUTES MEASURED ON PARIA RIVER
THREE FLASH FLOOD WAVES MOVED THROUGH JACKS CANYON ON AUGUST 21. 1245-1430...1430-1530 AND 1630 UNTIL AROUND 1845. HALF INCH HAIL AND STORNG WIND WAS ALSO REPORTED.
ROAD CREWS WERE CLEANING UP MUD AND DEBRIS FROM FLASH FLOODING ON THE YOUNG-HEBER ROAD.
MUD AND ROCK SLIDE OVER THE 512 ROAD YOUNG-HEBER ROAD. CREWS WERE CALLEDTO CLEAR MUD AND DEBRIS FROM FLASH FLOODING.
TWO FEET OF WATER OVER NAVAJO ROUTE 15 NEAR CORNFIELDS
ST. JOHNS LAW ENFORCEMENT ALSO REPORTED TREES BLOWN DOWN IN TOWN.
HWY 264 WASHED OUT AT MILE MARKER 323
